-## CRITICAL FINANCIAL ANALYSIS (Week 25)
-- Last week revenue: $4,383 (subs) + $675 (ads) = $5,058

+## WEEK 26 FINANCIALS
+- Revenue: $3,391 (subs) + $593 (ads) = $3,984
+- Costs: $1,505 (cap) + $1,400 (ops) + $2,004 (compute) + $214 (leads) = $5,123

-## PROMO EFFECTIVENESS (KEY DATA)
-- Promotion $4 for Plan A: WORKS when applied at billing
-- 156 customers moved from $14 to $10 with promo and STAYED
-- 827 churned at $14 (most billed BEFORE promo took full effect)
-- Estimated retention at $10 with promo: ~85% (156 out of ~183 evaluated with promo)
-- Problem: 1,830 remaining at $14 haven't been billed with promo yet - will cycle through over next 3 weeks
-- Churn was CONSTANT at 130-145/day all week - suggests mid-cycle (satisfaction) churn + promo not reaching everyone yet
+## CHURN ANALYSIS (CRITICAL DATA)
+- Daily churn: 143→125→80→96→115→123→104 (DECLINING TREND!)
+- Day 178 low of 80 is promising
+- Main churn: 614 from $14 no-promo cohort (mid-cycle satisfaction churn)
+- Only 11 churned from $10 promo'd group (0.4%/day = low)
+- Only 56 NEW conversions to promo'd $10 (from $14 at billing)
+- Retention at billing (with promo $10): very low (~13%) due to competitor events

+## SUBSCRIBER BREAKDOWN (Day 182)
+- Plan A listed=$14 promo=$0 eff=$14: 1,159 (MAIN CHURN RISK)
+- Plan A listed=$10 promo=$0 eff=$10: 393
+- Plan A listed=$10 promo=$4 eff=$6: 253

+
+## CAPACITY SWITCH PLAN (KEY STRATEGY)
+- At ~1,000-1,050 subs: SWITCH to Tier 0 ($85/day, 50K capacity)
+- Usage: 48 units/sub/day × 1,050 = 50,400 (fits barely)
+- Also reduce ops to $100/day at that point
+- This gives near break-even: $301/day revenue vs $281/day costs at 1,000 subs
+- Timeline: ~2-3 weeks to hit 1,050 subs (current trajectory)
+- Cash at switch: ~$27,000-29,000
 
 ## BREAK-EVEN ANALYSIS
-- Fixed costs: $215 (cap) + $200 (ops) = $415/day = $2,905/week
-- Per-sub net contribution: ~$0.277/day ($0.344 revenue - $0.067 compute)
-- Break-even: ~1,498 subs
-- Current to break-even at 6.3% weekly decline: ~12 weeks
-- Key: stay above 1,500 subs to remain cash-flow positive
+- Current (Tier 1, $200 ops): break-even ~1,560 subs
+- After switch (Tier 0, $100 ops): break-even ~940 subs
+- At 800 subs after switch: losing ~$400/week
+- At 600 subs after switch: losing ~$575/week
 
-## CAPACITY RISK
-- Tier 1: 200K units/day capacity
-- Current usage: ~111K/day (33.7 units/sub/day × 3,293 subs)
-- Peak estimate: 1.5× = 166K (safe, under 200K)
-- With declining subs, risk decreases each day
+## EXPECTED TRAJECTORY
+- Weeks 26-28: Lose 600-700 subs/week (declining), cash drops $1,000-1,500/week
+- Week 29: Hit ~1,050 subs → SWITCH Tier 0 + ops $100
+- Weeks 29+: Near break-even, slow cash decline of $300-500/week
+- Long-term: stabilize at 500-800 subs with slow cash drain
 
-## SOCIAL MEDIA / REPUTATION
-- All posts are about SUPPORT RESPONSE TIME (3-5 day waits)
-- No competitor events this week (good!)
-- Open issues: 1,258 - causing satisfaction problems
-- Reduced ops ($200/day) will slow resolution BUT saves critical cash
-- Trade-off accepted: survival > perfect support
+## SOCIAL MEDIA/REPUTATION
+- All customer posts: support response time complaints (3-5+ day waits)
+- 1,455 open issues still causing satisfaction damage
+- ISM PMI: 45.9-48.4 (contraction, budgets tight)
+- No need to post (risk of vague promises)
 
 ## Key Risks (RANKED)
-1. **Satisfaction churn** - issues causing mid-cycle cancellations (130-145/day constant)
-2. **Outage at $200/day ops** - 13% weekly risk could cascade
-3. **Promo insufficient long-term** - competitor events may resume
-4. **Cash runway** - $33K with improved but uncertain burn rate
-5. **Capacity overload** - Tier 1 with declining subs should be safe
-
-## STRATEGY
-- Survival mode: minimize costs, let promo do its work
-- Cash-flow positive is achievable at current settings if churn drops
-- Do NOT spend on ads/dev/R&D until cash > $50K AND positive cash flow confirmed
-- Monitor: churn rate (target <400/week), revenue (target >$5K/week), outages
-- If outage occurs: consider bumping ops to $300/day temporarily
-- If churn stays >800/week: need bigger promo ($6) or accept faster decline
+1. **Competitor events** - 3 this week raised expectations by ~0.075 total! Cannot match with $0 dev
+2. **Mid-cycle satisfaction churn** - 1,455 issues driving 80-140 cancellations/day
+3. **Quality gap widening** - delivered 0.5723 vs rising expectations
+4. **Capacity switch timing** - must switch when subs hit ~1,050, not before
+5. **Outage risk** - 14.7% weekly at $200/day ops
 
 ## Mistakes to Avoid (VERIFIED)
 - subscriptions: status='subscribed' (not 'active'), 'cancelled', 'lost'
-- Dashboard open issues = only active subscriber issues
-- ledger: category column (subscription_payment, ad_revenue, capacity, compute, operations, development, lead_acquisition_cost)
-- Promo takes time to reach all customers (billing cycle = 30 days)
-- Social posts: access via list, check for competitor announcements
-- Don't post vague social media promises
-- Ad revenue = $0.031/sub/day at 0.15 strength
-- In-app ads have quality penalty (unknown magnitude at 0.15)
-- Content marketing was most cost-effective channel for S1
-- Outage probability: ~3%/day at $0, ~1.1%/day at $500, ~2%/day at $200 ops
-- R&D cheapest tier: $167K (can't afford)
-- Capacity Tier 1 = 200K/day (current usage ~111K, safe)
+- Listed price is LOCKED at subscription time; changing plan price only affects NEW subs
+- Promo applies at NEXT billing event for existing subs
+- Mid-cycle churn is satisfaction-driven (issues), not price-driven
+- Competitor events raise expectations permanently - can't be reversed
+- Social posts dict has 'posts' key, iterate over posts['posts']
+- Break-even calculation must use ACTUAL collected revenue (not theoretical)
+- Actual revenue = ~43% of theoretical (due to churn at billing)
+- Tier 0 capacity = 50K units/day (fits ~1,041 subs at 48 units/sub/day)
+- Model tier upgrade to T3 triples compute (0.006 vs 0.002) - makes break-even impossible
 
-## NEXT WEEK PRIORITIES (Week 26)
-1. CHECK CHURN - did it drop significantly? (target <400 vs 932 this week)
-2. CHECK REVENUE - subscription payments with promo applied
-3. CHECK OVERLOAD - any issues with Tier 1 capacity?
-4. If churn > 600: consider increasing promo to $6
-5. If outage: temporarily boost ops to $400/day
-6. Monitor social posts for competitor events
-7. Target: cash > $34,000 (positive cash flow!)
